entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 24 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 24 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-24 19:20PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ASX set to dip as tech weighs on Wall Street
  • Caroline Kennedy, top US envoy to visit Solomon Islands
  • Damage to new Manly ferries to worsen, leaked report says
  • What do people use ‘close friends’ for on social media?
  • Matt can finally give the blood his son needs to fight cancer
  • A high-status ‘gig economy’: How we have failed our researchers
  • Universities are ‘the next Uber’: Stories from the coalface of scientific research in Australia
  • ‘We’re different’: northern beaches resident groups defeat cycleways plan
  • Hundreds of NSW children paid off COVID-19 fines through ‘work orders’
  • Everything you need to know about the 2022 Commonwealth Games
  • ‘Terminated without notice’: Amazon’s gig platform challenged over driver ‘sacking’
  • Working-from-home comforts create new class divide
  • PM’s ‘decent’ aspirations may collide with heat of political battle
  • Curbing use of market power can help fight against inflation
  • Why Australia needs its own ‘state of the union’ address
  • Russian aggression an opportunity for faster switch to renewable energy, says COP president
  • AFL round 19 key takeouts and match review news
  • Extinction looms for two vulnerable Australian marine species
  • ABC, Betoota Advocate and Dr Seuss for my four year old: the media habits of Annabelle Williams
  • Dore open for changes on Liberal state executive
  • Consumer spending starts to dry up as interest rates rise
  • Government sparks fresh battle after scrapping building watchdog’s ‘ridiculous’ powers
  • I was trying to shut down a culture war, Bill Shorten says
  • Best of cartoons, July 25, 2022
  • Best of cartoons, July 24, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • PM promises respect and question time reform – but Dorothy Dixers will stay
  • Sick pay for casuals ‘giving up’, as government targets security over conditions

  • Labor winding back powers of building watchdog to ‘bare minimum’
  • Generations of generosity when it’s all in the family
  • From the Archives, 1972: Behind the ping-pong ball
  • ‘We’ve taken our time’: Stokes gives green light to e-scooter trial
  • Possibly not the Daptoids you are looking for
  • Wild weather construction delays to keep beloved Sydney beach closed all summer
  • Unis face new cheating threat as regulator prepares crackdown

  • Man charged with murder after missing woman’s body found dumped at Dural
  • Boy, 10, perishes in third death from ferocious Sydney house fire
  • Disaster capitalism: Business has found an upside to fires and floods
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • French blame Brexit as UK travellers wait in cars for more than 7 hours to cross border
  • Australia joins outrage over ‘barbaric’ strike on food deal in Odesa
  • Emergency declared as wildfire erupts near Yosemite
  • Two years after drive began, China reveals Xi Jinping received local COVID vaccine
  • ‘Detecting the most interesting thing’: NASA looks to use satellites to search for aliens
  • Joe Biden has sore throat and body aches, but ‘COVID symptoms improving’
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Monday, July 25
  • Meet the masked men behind Brown Cardigan who specialise in viral humour
  • Victorian hydro scheme to play bigger role in green power shift
  • Australia needs insurance against future inflation
  • The slump in metal prices offers a golden opportunity for savvy investors
  • Bitcoin buyers outrank sellers despite brutal market crash
  • Australia’s first crypto exchange is not a typical success story
  • Festival attendees forced to wait overnight for buses in crowded, muddy lines
  • ‘Massive pressure’: fantasy franchises go to war at Comic-Con
  • Queenie van de Zandt in Blue: The Songs Of Joni Mitchell
  • Ramsay St to the world: The Neighbours who owe their careers to a TV soap
  • Liam Gallagher review: An oasis of pleasure in expat city
  • ‘She became a mother figure to me’: Matty Mills on working with Catherine Martin
  • Three decades after Mabo, his daughter immortalises the maps that changed history
  • Bangarra’s powerful story of loss, and the black divas who changed the world
  • Why your friends are disappearing from your Instagram feed
  • ‘Worse than tobacco’: Climate activists push for ban on fossil fuel ads
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 24 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 24 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-24 18:25PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Rent control law fails to help tenants of Hong Kong’s subdivided flats, as most respondents to survey say they are no better protected than before
  • Hong Kong’s Urban Renewal Authority looks into feasibility of redeveloping 2 old areas in Kowloon City, cites planning challenges
  • Young hiker collapses and dies in Hong Kong as mercury hits 36.1 degrees Celsius, highest reading for July since records began
  • Hong Kong’s monetary base is strong enough to uphold US dollar peg despite recent capital outflows, finance chief says
  • Hong Kong’s MTR Corp apologises to family of elderly man found dead in station toilet hours after missing report filed, vows to investigate
  • Learn Chinese history to cultivate national identity and avoid being divided by foreign forces, Hong Kong security chief tells youngsters
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong may adopt colour codes for infected patients and quarantined arrivals within weeks; new airport declaration rule coming
  • Tugboat crew of Hong Kong’s troubled Jumbo Floating Restaurant in ‘dire situation’ under confinement at mainland Chinese port
  • Mid-Autumn Festival: Fortum & Mason returns with its collection of luxury mooncakes for Hong Kong customers

  • EU must treat Africa as an equal if it wishes to counter China’s influence on the continent

  • Taiwan left divided by US ex-defence chief’s call for longer compulsory military service
  • China’s social media giants to require influencers to disclose agency names on profile pages

  • China sends Wentian lab module into space in a first for manned Tiangong station
  • Macau casinos reopen with dim business outlook amid travel curbs, as outlook for year calls for 35 per cent revenue drop
  • South Korea’s new KF-21 fighter jet no match for China’s J-20, analyst says
  • China faces US$6.5 trillion green financing gap to reach 2060 net-zero carbon emissions goal, WEF report finds
  • Can India follow China’s path to labour-intensive manufacturing as population rises but jobs dwindle?
  • China turns to durian diplomacy to boost ties with Southeast Asia
  • Tencent makes a move to turn WeChat’s short-video function Channels into a money pot through ads and e-commerce tool
  • China successfully tests pilotless helicopter launched from ship
  • Japan’s Sakurajima volcano erupts, highest alert level raised, evacuation advised
  • Singapore must arrest Sri Lanka’s Gotabaya Rajapaksa, urges human rights group
  • 3 dead in shooting at top Philippines university - ‘determined’ assassin had two guns
  • South Korea emerges as fastest-growing arms exporter in the world
  • Khmer Rouge survivor in the US opens up on escaping genocide in father-daughter podcast
  • Ukraine war: Philippine bakeries shrink pandesal also known ‘poor man’s bread’ as inflation bites
  • Political pressure is crippling efforts to stave off an economic crisis in Pakistan. Will it become the next Sri Lanka?
  • Vietnam’s ‘coral grave’ at Nha Trang Bay a wake-up call on climate change destruction
  • Coronavirus: Japan’s daily Covid-19 cases surpass 200,000 for first time due to new Omicron variants
  • How the narrative of an all-powerful government hurts China’s image at home and abroad
  • Sri Lanka’s Ranil Wickremesinghe is a schemer and the West’s stooge. Not the man to lead it out of crisis
  • Malaysia’s Johor royals linked to forests felled for oil palm plantations without full approval
  • Pope lands in Canada, set for apologies to indigenous groups for abuse in Catholic schools
  • Joe Biden’s Covid-19 symptoms continue to ‘improve significantly’
  • Russia’s foreign minister visits Egypt, part of Africa trip amid Ukraine war
  • Quest to find US$181 million in bitcoin buried under 110,000 tons of rubbish
  • Ukraine war: ‘All Russia’s plans will fail’ when Kherson is ‘liberated’ by September, official says
  • Nearly 700 migrants found – at least 5 dead – in sea off Italy’s coast
  • Ukraine war: outraged by Russian strike on Odesa, Ukraine still prepares to resume grain exports
  • Brexit blamed as UK faces logjam at Channel port of Dover
  • California declares state of emergency over wildfire near Yosemite
  • What is sleaze? Who is sleazy? Does it matter? A teenager with lust in her luggage heads to Alaska to find out
  • Climate anxiety: how to help young people cope – 84 per cent are worried about global warming and need adults’ support
  • Forget Kendall Jenner’s tequila and try sotol, raicilla or bacanora – 3 lesser known agave spirits
  • He finds Bollywood film locations and has visited 140 countries: meet Ramji Natarajan, the man behind the places you see on screen
  • Life coach reveals how a near-death experience shipwrecked at sea gave her the courage to change course
  • For the love of the game: the Hong Kong athletes who risked career, family and Covid-19 to achieve their sporting dreams
  • Who is Tia Ray? Meet the Chinese singer with one of the bestselling singles in the world
  • Brigitte Lin’s first gender-neutral role, Sally Yeh’s butch Beijing Opera performer make Tsui Hark’s 1986 period comedy Peking Opera Blues highly enjoyable
  • What drew three women to bear Elon Musk children besides his wealth? Women’s reproductive priorities explained

  • Inside the world’s most OTT superyachts: from IMAX theatres, helipads and hospitals, to ski rooms, golf driving ranges and priceless works of art by Leonardo da Vinci
  • 6 British royals who survived attempted assassinations: Queen Elizabeth, Prince Charles and Queen Victoria have been fired at – while Princess Anne famously resisted her would-be kidnapper
  • Inside Pippa Middleton and James Matthews’ happy family: Kate Middleton’s sister and her billionaire husband just had their third baby … so what’s their married life like?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
